understanding: A youth employment training programme tracks 30 participants across three survey waves (Baseline, Midline, Endline). The table records enrolment, training completion, employment status, monthly income, household size and satisfaction — enough to measure reach, income progression and employment outcomes over time.

tables.rationale: One row per participant per wave with outcome fields.

proposedMetrics.name: Participants enrolled
proposedMetrics.description: Distinct participants that appear in the programme records.

proposedMetrics.name: Average monthly income at endline
proposedMetrics.description: Mean reported monthly income (USD) among endline responses.
assumptions: Self-reported income is accurate to the month.
caveats: Some income values are missing and are excluded, not zeroed.

proposedMetrics.name: Employment rate at endline
proposedMetrics.description: Share of endline respondents reporting formal employment.
caveats: Counts respondents, not all enrolled participants.

proposedMetrics.name: Average satisfaction by wave
proposedMetrics.description: Mean satisfaction score (1-5) per survey wave.
assumptions: The 1-5 scale is comparable across waves.

activity.rationale: training_completed records participation in the core activity.

output.rationale: Completion counts are direct programme outputs.

outcome.rationale: Employment status and income capture participant outcomes.

impact.rationale: Household size hints at wider reach, but no counterfactual exists.

what.rationale: Participants moved from unemployment into paid work, with monthly income tracked as the measure of change.

who.rationale: 31 young jobseekers, tracked individually across three regions.

howMuch.rationale: Employment and income were measured at three points in time (baseline, midline, endline), showing how much changed and whether it lasted.

contribution.rationale: Unknown. There is no comparison group, so the data can't show whether participants would have found work anyway.

risk.rationale: The numbers are self-reported; free-text feedback mentions dropout and attendance problems that could bias the results.

frameworkTags.label: Decent Work and Economic Growth
frameworkTags.rationale: Employment and income outcomes align with decent-work goals.
frameworkTags.caveat: Candidate alignment only — not a compliance assessment.

uncertainties: Income is self-reported and missing for some respondents.
Endline non-response may bias employment rate upward.
